original_hypothesis.title: AgentNftV2 setDAO Can Permanently Brick Agent Governance by Setting DAO to address(0)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setDAO() allows the current DAO to change itself to any address including address(0), with no validation. If a malicious DAO proposal passes that calls setDAO(virtualId, address(0)), the agent's governance becomes permanently bricked since all onlyVirtualDAO-gated functions require msg.sender == address(0), which is impossible.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The onlyVirtualDAO modifier checks _msgSender() == virtualInfos[virtualId].dao. If dao is set to address(0), no caller can ever satisfy this condition. setCoreTypes, setTokenURI, and setDAO itself all become permanently inaccessible.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setDAO performs no validation on newDAO address; require(_msgSender() == virtualInfos[virtualId].dao) in modifier becomes unsatisfiable if dao == address(0)

preconditions: Attacker gains DAO governance majority (via OPS-H001 delegation hijack or legitimate voting power)
Malicious proposal to call AgentNftV2.setDAO(virtualId, address(0)) passes

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: setDAO has no require(newDAO != address(0)) and no require(newDAO is valid governor)
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time governance can be captured

operational_questions: Can ADMIN_ROLE override via migrateVirtual to recover from address(0) DAO?
What is the minimum voting power needed to pass a setDAO proposal?
Can this be chained with OPS-H001 for a low-cost governance bricking attack?
validation_requirements: Pass DAO proposal calling setDAO(virtualId, address(0))
Verify all onlyVirtualDAO functions become permanently inaccessible
Check if migrateVirtual can restore governance

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Gain DAO voting majority (via OPS-H001 or token accumulation)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Submit proposal to call setDAO(virtualId, address(0))
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Vote proposal to pass and execute
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Agent governance is permanently bricked - no metadata updates possible
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Sufficient voting power; can be obtained via OPS-H001

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Cannot update agent metadata, core types, or token URI
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Only recoverable if ADMIN_ROLE holder calls migrateVirtual
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: setCoreTypes, setTokenURI, setDAO - all permanently blocked

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: DAO proposals targeting setDAO with address(0) argument
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: DAO address changing to zero for any virtualId
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Delegation hijacking followed by governance proposals

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
